stories.title: North Korea Will ‘Never’ Get Nuclear Recognition, EU, Seoul Say
stories.summary: South Korea and the European Union said North Korea will “never” be recognized as a nuclear-weapon state, reaffirming their commitment to denuclearization days after China and North Korea pledged closer ties at a summit that made no public mention of the issue.

stories.title: US launches fresh wave of strikes on Iran
stories.summary: Oil price rises as Washington and Tehran send mixed signals over status of the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Upstart chipmakers keep challenging Nvidia. This time it's Microsoft-backed D-Matrix
stories.summary: Nvidia challenger D-Matrix is entering full production of an AI chip it says is 10 times faster than a GPU and bypasses the memory shortage.

stories.title: Global Junk Debt Flashes Warning on Growing Risk of Stagflation
stories.summary: Fears of a stagflation shock from the Middle East conflict are increasingly souring investor sentiment toward the weakest global corporate borrowers, many of which binged on cheap debt during the era of ultra-low interest rates.

stories.title: Rep. Mackenzie Urges Iran Talks, Says Military Option Must Remain Open
stories.summary: Representative Ryan Mackenzie of Pennsylvania says Congress cannot let FISA lapse, arguing a short-term extension is the most likely outcome but a longer-term fix is needed to give the intelligence community stability. Rep. Mackenzie also says the US should seek a negotiated peace with Iran while keeping military action "always on the table," and says Americans need more relief from inflation, aft

stories.title: Oil Surges as Fresh US Strikes on Iran Threaten Fragile Truce
stories.summary: Oil jumped after a second day of US military strikes on Iran, with the Islamic Republic announcing a halt to all vessels through the Strait of Hormuz in response, putting further strain on a fragile ceasefire.

stories.title: US Inflation Picks Up to 3 Year High, Eroding Paychecks| Bloomberg Businessweek Daily 6/10/2026
stories.summary: Today, Chris Kennedy, Bloomberg Economics lead for economic statecraft, breaks down the state of negotiations between the US and Iran and touches on the impact of pushback to Trump's Iran war policies at the ballot box. stories.title: Trump keeps saying an Iran deal is close. Markets keep believing it
stories.summary: Analysts still assume that, despite the continued conflict, a deal will eventually be reached that ends the war and reopens the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: XAI Co-Founder Babuschkin Unveils New Startup for Personalized AI
stories.summary: A team of former employees from Elon Musk’s xAI have formed a new startup focused on personalized artificial intelligence, marking perhaps the most high-profile new venture yet from the wave of recent xAI departures.

stories.title: Oracle's Earnings 'Really Strong,' RBC's Jaluria Says
stories.summary: Rishi Jaluria, RBC Capital Markets software equity research managing director, reacts to Oracle's fourth-quarter earnings. Speaking on "Bloomberg The Close," Jaluria says Oracle is a bit of a proxy for the AI infrastructure buildout trade. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Oracle Falls After Data Center Costs Overshadow AI Growth
stories.summary: Oracle Corp. shares declined in extended trading after the company reported quarterly capital expenses that were higher than estimates, raising investor concerns about the profitability of the AI infrastructure business.

stories.title: Citi Says Investors Growing More Selective on Data Center Bonds
stories.summary: Bond investors are increasingly scrutinizing financing deals linked to artificial intelligence infrastructure buildouts, according to Citigroup Inc. analysts.

stories.title: U.S. military completes strikes on 'multiple targets' in Iran launched at Trump's direction
stories.summary: President Donald Trump promised more attacks on Iran after strikes resumed this week. Earlier this week, he said a deal to end the war was near.

stories.title: CNBC Daily Open: Trump expresses 'love' for inflation print and anger at Iran
stories.summary: U.S. inflation reached a three-year high of 4.2%, with Trump arguing that ending the war will resolve both the conflict and rising prices.

stories.summary: The global energy crisis has helped propel BYD back into pole position in China’s automotive sector, allowing it to regain ground on Geely Auto as demand for battery-powered vehicles accelerates worldwide. The Shenzhen-based electric vehicle (EV) maker, which lost its title as mainland China’s largest carmaker in the first quarter of 2026, rebounded strongly in the following two months as the US-I

stories.title: Global AI trade flashing signals reminiscent of the dotcom bust, analysts warn
stories.summary: The global artificial intelligence trade is flashing signals reminiscent of past market tops, with winners outpacing the broader market by a wide margin and a slew of jumbo initial public offerings (IPOs) in the pipeline. The gap between richly valued stocks and cheaper ones had reached extremes seen only before the dotcom bust in March 2000, according to Bank of America. At the same time, the pac

stories.title: Lee Raymond, Exxon CEO Who Doubted Climate Change, Dies at 87
stories.summary: Lee Raymond, the former head of Exxon Mobil Corp. who oversaw the biggest corporate merger in the history of the oil industry and was derided as “the Darth Vader of global warming” for his skepticism toward climate change, has died. He was 87.

stories.title: Sen. Scott Backs FISA Extension, Pushes Surveillance Reforms
stories.summary: Senator Rick Scott of Florida says he will vote for a short-term FISA extension, but argues Congress should stop "kicking the can down the road" and enact reforms to the surveillance law. He downplays criticism of Bill Pulte's appointment as acting DNI, despite Pulte's lack of intelligence experience. Sen. Scott also says President Trump may have to use the military against Iran if Tehran refuses

stories.title: Gold Declines for Third Day After US Launches New Iran Strikes
stories.summary: Gold fell for a third day after the US launched fresh strikes against Iran, threatening to extend the war that’s roiled global markets and stoked inflation.

stories.summary: Paul Andre Huet, chairman & CEO of Americas Gold & Silver, and Gary Evans, CEO of US Antimony, discussed the impact of war with Iran on US critical minerals supplies and how the ramp up in tech IPOs has increased demand, even as metals prices have slumped in recent days. stories.title: US Strikes Iran in Trump Escalation Over Stalled Peace Talks
stories.summary: The American military launched strikes against “multiple” targets in Iran for the second straight day after President Donald Trump accused the country of dragging out talks on an interim peace deal.

Hong Kong’s pharmaceutical sector is navigating one of its most complex transitions in dec

stories.title: OpenAI Says China-Linked Accounts Aim to Fuel US Data Center Pushback
stories.summary: OpenAI said a group of ChatGPT accounts with ties to China have sought to stir up local opposition to data centers in the US in a potential bid to hinder the country’s competitiveness in artificial intelligence, echoing recent rhetoric from others in the tech industry.

stories.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says U.S. will attack Iran 'very hard'
stories.summary: Oil prices rose after the U.S. launched military strikes against Iran, raising concerns that renewed clashes may threaten shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Anthropic CEO Says Government Should Be Able to Block New Models
stories.summary: Anthropic PBC Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei said the government should have the power to block artificial intelligence developers from deploying new AI models if they present certain risks.

stories.title: AI Data Firm DDN Eyeing a Fresh Funding Round by End of Year
stories.summary: DDN, a data hardware and software provider that counts Nvidia Corp. as a partner and Alphabet Inc.’s Google and Salesforce Inc. as customers, is looking to raise additional funding by the end of the year, Chief Executive Officer Alex Bouzari said.

stories.title: Wall Street needs a crash course in the token economy ahead of AI IPOs. SpaceX offers a preview
stories.summary: When OpenAI and Anthropic make their IPO prospectuses available to the public, investors are going to have to learn about a whole new economy.

stories.title: Apollo Wraps Up $35B Chip Deal for Anthropic
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Emily Graffeo joins Matt Miller on "Bloomberg Deals." Apollo Global Management and Blackstone have finalized a $35 billion financing package for Anthropic to expand its AI infrastructure, marking the latest mega-deal in the AI race. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: North Carolina treasurer passes on SpaceX citing valuation concerns; favors OpenAI, Anthropic
stories.summary: North Carolina has invested heavily in AI startups OpenAI and Anthropic while avoiding a direct stake in SpaceX.

stories.title: AI Is Not Safe Yet, Says UCLA Professor
stories.summary: As AI giants Anthropic and OpenAI prepare for IPOs, the companies are stressing their efforts to ensure the tech is safe and beneficial for humanity. But Safiya Noble, a UCLA professor and director of the university’s Center on Resilience & Digital Justice, says current AI is not safe, and that stereotypes and biases are being built into training data. Noble joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on “B

stories.title: Texas Governor Seeks Consumer Protection From Data Center Costs
stories.summary: Texas Governor Greg Abbott called on state regulators to better protect ratepayers from shouldering the costs of the data center buildout, the latest sign of growing political scrutiny over the AI-driven surge in electricity demand.

stories.title: SpaceX IPO Multiple Times Oversubscribed | Bloomberg Tech 6/10/2026
stories.summary: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss Wall Street’s enthusiasm for SpaceX as the company’s IPO approaches. Plus, Google backstops Anthropic data centers as Silicon Valley races to build AI infrastructure with ever more intertwined deals. And, a new dashboard shows the real-time impact of AI on the labor market. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Why Sustainable Finance Hasn’t Moved the Needle on Climate Change

stories.title: Danantara Starts Bond Sale in Test After Indonesian Selloff
stories.summary: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund Danantara kicked off its debut global dollar bond sale, testing investor appetite at a time when confidence in Southeast Asia’s largest economy has soured.

stories.title: Thai Long Bonds Draw Funds With Steepest Curve in Emerging Asia
stories.summary: Thailand’s yield curve has shifted to the steepest in emerging Asia, with some investors saying that makes its longer-dated bonds look attractive given a divergence in interest-rate expectations there versus regional peers.

stories.title: SpaceX IPO Is Said to Be Over Four Times Oversubscribed
stories.summary: SpaceX's initial public offering has attracted demand for more than four times the available shares, according to people familiar with the matter. Bloomberg's Michael Hytha breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Investor Tied to Troubled Bank Loans Charged With Fraud by US
stories.summary: A California real estate investor whose firm was accused by Western Alliance Bancorp of an almost $100 million fraud was criminally charged by the US, the latest fallout from a portfolio of commercial properties that sparked credit concerns at several banks last year.

stories.title: Inside India newsletter: Hollywood is debating AI. India's filmmakers are embracing it
stories.summary: Even as Hollywood remains the world's most influential storyteller, experiments in AI filmmaking are increasingly happening in India.

stories.title: Air India Crash Report to Miss One-Year Deadline With Engine Probe Pending
stories.summary: Investigators will miss Friday’s one-year deadline to explain why an Air India Ltd. jetliner crashed right after takeoff because an examination of the Boeing Co. plane’s engines in the US still needs to be completed, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.title: Oil jumps as U.S. fresh strikes on Iran raise worries of extended disruption to energy flows
stories.summary: Oil prices jumped on Thursday after the United States launched a fresh round of military strikes against targets in Iran.

stories.title: Activist Elliott Hits Back at Gold Miner Northern Star Over Sale
stories.summary: Activist investor Elliott Investment Management LP has hit back at Australia’s biggest gold stock Northern Star Resources Ltd. by urging the beleaguered miner’s board to take urgent action and reconsider a sale as its valuation flounders.

stories.title: New Zealand Primary Exports Seen Slower After Stellar 2026 Gain
stories.summary: New Zealand’s target of doubling primary exports by 2034 remains in place even as the stellar growth of recent years is tipped to slow in the face of geopolitical and weather challenges.

stories.title: US Launches Second Round of Strikes on Iran
stories.summary: The US military launched strikes against "multiple" targets in Iran for the second day in a row after President Donald Trump accused the country of dragging out talks on an interim peace deal. Bloomberg's Laura Davison explains the context. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Westpac Mortgage Applications Fall as Tax Changes Sap Demand

stories.summary: Veteran short seller James Chanos said SpaceX’s blockbuster public debut is being driven more by investor enthusiasm for Elon Musk and artificial intelligence than by financial fundamentals, arguing the company’s valuation is difficult to justify on any reasonable business assumptions.

stories.title: Florida Property Tax Cut Could Strain Some Local Debt, S&P Says
stories.summary: Local governments in Florida will face “greater credit pressure” if voters approve a plan to largely eliminate property taxes on more than half of homes in the state, according to analysts at S&P Global Ratings.
